SMRotationDisabled.^description: Automatic rotation is not enabled for this secret. Rotating secrets regularly limits the damage from compromised credentials. AWS Secrets Manager can automatically rotate credentials for supported AWS services and custom secrets using Lambda functions.

SMRotationOverdue.^description: This secret has not been rotated in over 90 days despite rotation being enabled. Check if the rotation Lambda function is working correctly and that the rotation schedule is appropriate for your security requirements.

SMNoCustomKMS.^description: This secret is encrypted with the default AWS-managed key (aws/secretsmanager) instead of a customer-managed KMS key. Using a customer-managed key provides greater control including key rotation, access auditing, and the ability to revoke access by disabling the key.
SMNoCustomKMS.shortDesc: Use customer-managed KMS key for secret encryption

SMSecretUnused.^description: This secret has not been accessed in over 90 days. Unused secrets still incur charges and represent unnecessary attack surface. Review whether this secret is still needed and delete it if not.

SMSecretNeverAccessed.^description: This secret has never been accessed since creation. It may have been created in error or is no longer needed. Review and delete unused secrets to reduce costs and attack surface.
SMSecretNeverAccessed.shortDesc: Delete secrets that have never been accessed
